helpful: Provisioned petition.txt included the full Ninth Circuit memorandum decision in the appendix — no external fetch needed for the decision under review.
Per-Term paid-vs-IFP grant rates in statpack.json gave the right base-rate anchor for a paid petition.
CourtListener MCP confirmed the petition's key intervening authority (Zorn v. Linton, per curiam, 2026-03-23) in two calls.
gaps: The prompt's 'Segment base rate by salience band' statpack section does not exist in the committed metrics/statpack.md or statpack.json, so no band anchor was available; adjusted from the call-for-response signal manually.
fedcourts query has no free-text or subject filter usable on SCOTUS rows (--topic is circuit-only), so pulling comparable qualified-immunity cert priors from the corpus was not possible.

No statpack cut for call-for-response (CFR) status — empirically one of the strongest pre-conference grant signals; a CFR bucket alongside the relist/CVSG cuts would directly serve petitions like this one.
CourtListener MCP rate limit (1200/day, shared) returned 429s on 2 of 5 calls; short waits resolved them but a backoff hint in the error would help.
notes: Forward cell; ~1 corpus query + 4 MCP calls, well under the 25-call budget.
